title: Intense Copper Peruvian Tofu Stew
description: A bitter yet tender stew that showcases Peruvian flavors, featuring sous-vide tofu, vegan butter, and a copper-pot finish.
instructions: Vacuum seal cubed tofu with soy sauce, salt, and pepper, then cook sous‑vide at 25C for 45 min.
While tofu cooks, melt vegan butter in a copper pot over medium heat until foamy.
Add diced onion to the pot and sauté until translucent, about 5 min.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Toss in cubed pumpkin and cook for 3 min, stirring frequently.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable stock, then stir in aji amarillo paste and bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let the sauce thicken for 10 min, stirring occasionally.
Add the sous‑vide tofu to the pot, gently heat through for 5 min without breaking the cubes.
Finish the stew with chopped cilantro, lime juice, and a pinch of pepper, adjusting salt as needed.
Serve hot, spooning the stew into bowls and garnishing with extra cilantro if desired.
